action.reasoning: Observation: All heuristic controllers (PID, waypoint following) yield safe_follow_steps near zero; robot never gets close enough while facing human simultaneously. Analysis: The robot likely gets stuck behind obstacles because we haven't used depth for obstacle avoidance. Literature Heuristic Expert (navmesh shortest path) works because it uses a global planner; our navmesh pathfinder often returns empty waypoints. A reactive controller using head_depth for avoidance + GPS for navigation may work better than the current failing pathfinder. Decision: Implement reactive controller with depth-based obstacle avoidance and GPS heading, test on 3 episodes. If safe_follow improves significantly, submit eval. Otherwise consider lightweight RL (PPO with <1M steps) as a last resort.
